title: Conditionals & Variables
description: What if-blocks and variables are in Scratch, with four hands-on builds: bouncing ball, score counter, if/else check, and a colour-change reaction game.

steps.title: Do Now Activity
steps.content:
Do Now

Which block category contains forever and if blocks?
steps.js: createMCQ('cv-dna',['Control (orange)','Motion (blue)','Looks (purple)','Sensing (light blue)'],0,'Correct — Control blocks (orange) contain loops and if/else.','Control blocks are orange. They contain forever, repeat, if, and if/else.');

steps.title: Conditional Statements
steps.content:
Conditional statements

A conditional statement runs code only if a condition is true. In Scratch, use the if block (orange).

if <touching [edge v]?> then
  turn right (180) degrees
end

The condition is the hexagonal block inside the if — it is either true or false. Sensing blocks (light blue) give you conditions like touching edge? and key pressed?.

if <key [space v] pressed?> then
  say [Space pressed!] for (1) seconds
end

The if/else version runs one block of code when true and a different block when false:

if <touching [edge v]?> then
  say [Edge!] for (1) seconds
else
  move (5) steps
end

steps.title: Build 1: Bouncing Ball
steps.content:
Build 1: bouncing ball

Build a sprite that moves continuously and bounces when it hits the edge. The if on edge, bounce block handles this automatically.

when green flag clicked
forever
  move (5) steps
  if on edge, bounce
end
  1. when green flag clicked (Events)
  2. forever loop (Control)
  3. Inside: move (5) steps (Motion)
  4. Inside: if on edge, bounce (Motion)
  5. Run it — try increasing the speed number

f=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame'),r=document.getElementById('y7-check-result');try{var vm=f.contentWindow.vm;if(!vm){r.style.color='#fca5a5';r.textContent='Editor still loading.';return;}var T=vm.runtime.targets,hF=false,hM=false,hB=false;for(var t=0;t<T.length;t++){var B=T[t].blocks._blocks;for(var id in B){var b=B[id];if(b.opcode==='control_forever')hF=true;if(b.opcode==='motion_movesteps')hM=true;if(b.opcode==='motion_ifonedgebounce')hB=true;}}if(hF&&hM&&hB){r.style.color='#86efac';r.textContent='✓ Bouncing ball complete!';__markStepComplete();}else{var m=[];if(!hF)m.push('forever');if(!hM)m.push('move steps');if(!hB)m.push('if on edge, bounce');r.style.color='#fca5a5';r.textContent='Missing: '+m.join(', ');}}catch(e){r.style.color='#fca5a5';r.textContent='Error: '+e.message;}};})()

steps.title: Variables
steps.content:
Variables in Scratch

A variable is a named box that stores a value. In Scratch, variables are dark orange.

To create one: Variables → Make a Variable → type a name → OK

Three blocks you will use constantly:

set [score v] to (0)       ← give score a starting value
change [score v] by (1)    ← add 1 to score
change [score v] by (-1)   ← take 1 away from score

Always set the variable to 0 at the start, then change it as events happen in the game.

steps.title: Build 2: Score Counter
steps.content:
Build 2: score counter

Create a score that goes up when you press the space key and back to 0 when you press R.

when green flag clicked
set [score v] to (0)

when [space v] key pressed
change [score v] by (1)

when [r v] key pressed
set [score v] to (0)
  1. Variables → Make a Variable → name it score
  2. Build the three separate scripts above (three separate stacks)
  3. Run it — press Space to score, press R to reset

steps.title: Build 3: Bouncing Ball + Score
steps.content:
Build 3: bouncing ball with bounce counter

Combine the bouncing ball and a variable. Use an if block to detect the edge and count bounces.

when green flag clicked
set [bounces v] to (0)
forever
  move (5) steps
  if <touching [edge v]?> then
    change [bounces v] by (1)
    turn right (180) degrees
  end
end
  1. Create a variable called bounces
  2. Build the script above — the if block is in Control, touching edge? is in Sensing
  3. Run it and watch the bounce count rise

loading.';return;}var T=vm.runtime.targets,hIf=false,hChange=false,hSet=false,hSense=false,hForever=false;for(var t=0;t<T.length;t++){var B=T[t].blocks._blocks;for(var id in B){var b=B[id];if(b.opcode==='control_if'||b.opcode==='control_if_else')hIf=true;if(b.opcode==='data_changevariableby')hChange=true;if(b.opcode==='data_setvariableto')hSet=true;if(b.opcode==='sensing_touchingobject'||b.opcode==='motion_ifonedgebounce')hSense=true;if(b.opcode==='control_forever')hForever=true;}}if(hIf&&hChange&&hSet&&hForever){r.style.color='#86efac';r.textContent='✓ Bounce counter working!';__markStepComplete();}else{var m=[];if(!hForever)m.push('forever loop');if(!hIf)m.push('if block');if(!hSet)m.push('set variable');if(!hChange)m.push('change variable');r.style.color='#fca5a5';r.textContent='Missing: '+m.join(', ');}}catch(e){r.style.color='#fca5a5';r.textContent='Error: '+e.message;}};})()

steps.title: Build 4: Reaction Game
steps.content:
Build 4: colour reaction game

The sprite changes colour every 2 seconds. Press space when it is red to score a point. Use an if/else to check the colour.

when green flag clicked
set [score v] to (0)
forever
  set [color v] effect to (pick random (0) to (200))
  wait (2) seconds
end

when [space v] key pressed
if <(color effect) = (0)> then
  change [score v] by (1)
  say [Point!] for (0.5) seconds
else
  change [score v] by (-1)
  say [Wrong colour!] for (0.5) seconds
end

The colour effect goes 0-199 — choose a target number and press space at the right moment. You can simplify: just pressing space adds 1, any key press subtracts 1 if the colour is wrong.

tfs(){var f=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame'),w=f.parentElement,b=document.getElementById('y7-fs-btn'),fs=w.classList.toggle('y7-fullscreen');b.textContent=fs?'✕':'⛶';if(fs){window._sy=window.scrollY;document.body.style.overflow='hidden';}else{document.body.style.overflow='';window.scrollTo(0,window._sy||0);}sf();}sf();if(window._sfR)window.removeEventListener('resize',window._sfR);window._sfR=sf;window.addEventListener('resize',sf);if(window._sfK)document.removeEventListener('keydown',window._sfK);window._sfK=function(e){if(e.key==='Escape'){var f=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame');if(f&&f.parentElement.classList.contains('y7-fullscreen'))tfs();}};document.addEventListener('keydown',window._sfK);document.getElementById('y7-fs-btn').onclick=tfs;document.getElementById('y7-check-btn').onclick=function(){var f=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame'),r=document.getElementById('y7-check-result');try{var vm=f.contentWindow.vm;if(!vm){r.style.color='#fca5a5';r.textContent='Editor still loading.';return;}var T=vm.runtime.targets,hIf=false,hElse=false,hScore=false,hEffect=false,hKey=false;for(var t=0;t<T.length;t++){var B=T[t].blocks._blocks;for(var id in B){var b=B[id];if(b.opcode==='control_if_else')hElse=true;if(b.opcode==='control_if')hIf=true;if(b.opcode==='data_changevariableby')hScore=true;if(b.opcode==='looks_seteffectto'||b.opcode==='looks_changeeffectby')hEffect=true;if(b.opcode==='event_whenkeypressed')hKey=true;}}if((hIf||hElse)&&hScore&&hEffect&&hKey){r.style.color='#86efac';r.textContent='✓ Reaction game built!';__markStepComplete();}else{var m=[];if(!hIf&&!hElse)m.push('if block');if(!hScore)m.push('score variable');if(!hEffect)m.push('colour effect');if(!hKey)m.push('key press trigger');r.style.color='#fca5a5';r.textContent='Missing: '+m.join(', ');}}catch(e){r.style.color='#fca5a5';r.textContent='Error: '+e.message;}};})()

quizQuestions.q: What colour are Control blocks (loops and if/else)?
What does a conditional statement do?
options: Runs code only if a condition is true

quizQuestions.q: score is 5. 'change [score] by (1)' runs. What is score?
Which block gives a variable its starting value?
Which script makes a sprite bounce continuously?
options: when flag clicked → forever → move (5) steps → if on edge, bounce
when flag clicked → if on edge, bounce

quizQuestions.q: Build a script using a forever loop that moves the sprite and bounces it off the edge. Click Check & Submit.
quizQuestions.sampleAnswer: when flag clicked → forever → move 5 steps → if on edge, bounce

quizQuestions.q: Build a script that sets a variable to 0 when the flag is clicked, then changes it by 1 when space is pressed. Click Check & Submit.
quizQuestions.sampleAnswer: when flag → set [score] to 0 | when space → change [score] by 1

quizQuestions.q: Build a script that uses an if block to check if the sprite is touching the edge, and if so turns it around. Click Check & Submit.
quizQuestions.sampleAnswer: forever → if touching edge → turn 180 degrees
